nodes.name: spec.md
nodes.summary: Feature specification for Refactor TOC with 3 user stories: import full novel metadata with Hán Việt translation (P1), find and select chapters with sort/search/filter/range (P2), and per-chapter crawl/translate actions with override support (P3). Includes edge cases, functional requirements, key entities (NovelMetadata, Chapter, ChapterSelection, ChapterActionResult), success criteria, and assumptions.

nodes.name: data-model.md
nodes.summary: Formal data model for the refactored TOC defining four entities: NovelMetadata (title, author, description, source URL with original/translated dual fields), Chapter (index, title, URL, crawl/translate status), ChapterSelection (selected indices, range, filter criteria), and ChapterAction (crawl/translate per-chapter with override flag and result status).

nodes.name: research.md
nodes.summary: Technical research documenting 8 architectural decisions for the TOC refactor: (1) preserve original+displayed metadata in manifest, (2) use compatibility-tolerant manifest loading, (3) shared service logic for sort/filter/range, (4) reuse ebook overview chapter table as TOC table, (5) support both checked-row and visible-range targeting, (6) keep cache reuse default with explicit override boolean, (7) treat missing metadata as non-blocking visible status, (8) contract both CLI and Web UI interactions.

nodes.name: plan.md
nodes.summary: Implementation plan for Refactor TOC covering technical context (Python 3.10+, FastAPI, Jinja2, PyYAML, pytest), constitution check against project principles, project structure (specs docs vs source code layout), and complexity tracking. Defines source files affected: cli.py, toc.py, storage.py, config.py, pipeline.py, crawler.py, translator.py, epub_builder.py, and app/routes.

nodes.name: tasks.md
nodes.summary: Task breakdown in 6 phases: Setup (shared infrastructure), Foundational (blocking prerequisites), US1 P1 (import full metadata), US2 P2 (find/select chapters with sort/search/filter/range), US3 P3 (per-chapter crawl/translate actions with override), and Polish (cross-cutting concerns). Includes dependency graph, parallel opportunities per user story, and MVP-first incremental delivery strategy.

nodes.name: quickstart.md
nodes.summary: Step-by-step validation guide with CLI and Web UI workflows: fetch TOC metadata, translate metadata using Hán Việt rules, review chapter list controls, crawl a visible range without override, explicit override crawl/translate, Web UI start/fetch/review with sort/search/filter/checkboxes, and bulk per-chapter actions. Includes test commands for pytest validation.
